mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rate increase instances.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces household consumption expectations, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and declining inflation indicates robust real consumer demand without price pressures, expanding corporate profit margins and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day returns are negative when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that forces inventory drawdowns and risk-off positioning, producing negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations following industrial production declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, leading to negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns dominate when housing starts drop below the 1.2 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total employment and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows no statistical predictive power over 60-day windows (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL rising above 305.0 indicates accelerating price pressures that force central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive when CPI exceeds 305.0, or the signal fails to predict direction in >70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms macroeconomic expansion, reinforcing corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations.
